Function: Main hub for Western Regional Climate Center tools, data, and climate summaries.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, planners, ag extension agents, emergency managers, fire managers
Impact Evidence: Central WRCC hub; referenced in Western state climatologist workflows across AK, AZ, CA, CO, ID, MT, NV, NM, OR, UT, WA, WY and Pacific territories
Notes: Primary WRCC portal hosted at DRI; ACIS-backed; largest geographic coverage of any RCC including Pacific territories

Function: Interface for daily, monthly, and historical data queries across Western U.S. stations.
User Segment: Researchers, state climatologists, data analysts, NWS forecasters, water managers
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Western state drought monitoring and water resource planning workflows
Notes: Primary ACIS data interface for WRCC region; daily, monthly, and historical station queries

Function: View metadata, periods of record, and station information.
User Segment: Researchers, developers, data analysts, network administrators
Notes: Station metadata and period of record browser; ACIS-backed; useful for data provenance and QC workflows

Function: State and regional summaries with long-term climate normals and anomalies.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, state climatologists, educators, media
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Western state climate assessment documents
Notes: Historical climate summaries with long-term normals; NCEI has national equivalents but WRCC provides Western-specific depth

Function: Directory of state and regional service providers and climate offices in the West.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, general public
Notes: Service provider directory; static content; low restoration priority

Function: Interactive map of real-time temperature, precipitation, and snow data across the West.
User Segment: Emergency managers, NWS forecasters, fire managers, farmers, ranchers, water managers
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Western state emergency management real-time situational awareness workflows
Notes: Real-time conditions map integrating ACIS, RAWS, and mesonet data; unique multi-network integration for Western U.S.; no national equivalent at this breadth

Function: Displays departure-from-normal and percent-of-average maps.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, state agencies, media, farmers
Impact Evidence: Used in Western state drought and agricultural situation reports
Notes: Departure-from-normal and percent-of-average anomaly maps; core operational monitoring product for Western region

Function: Computes and maps SPI values for multiple time scales.
User Segment: Drought monitors, water resource managers, emergency managers, researchers
Impact Evidence: Feeds into NIDIS Western drought products; referenced in Western state drought contingency plans
Notes: SPI tool for multiple timescales; NDMC partial national substitute; WRCC version Western-framed with ACIS integration

Function: Regional drought dashboard linking to NIDIS, USDM, and SPI maps.
User Segment: Emergency managers, water resource managers, state agencies, farmers, ranchers
Impact Evidence: Embedded in NIDIS Western drought monitoring workflows
Notes: Regional drought dashboard linking NIDIS, USDM, and SPI; WRCC serves as key Western node in national drought monitoring architecture

Function: Provides access to RAWS network data for fire and rangeland management.
User Segment: Fire managers, incident commanders, rangeland managers, BLM/USFS staff, ranchers
Impact Evidence: RAWS data embedded in National Interagency Fire Center (NIFC) operational workflows; referenced in BLM and USFS fire weather protocols across Western states
Notes: RAWS portal is WRCC's most operationally critical fire weather asset; entirely independent of ACIS; feeds NIFC and NWS fire weather operations; no national substitute at this interface quality for Western incident support

Tool Name: SCENIC (Surface Climate and Evapotranspiration Network Information Center)
Function: Displays evapotranspiration and water balance data for western networks.
User Segment: Irrigation managers, water resource planners, ag extension agents, conservation districts
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Western water district irrigation scheduling guidance
Notes: ET and water balance data for Western networks; RAWS-dependent not ACIS; unique integration of ET modeling with Western station networks

Function: Climate and ET data viewer for California stations.
User Segment: CA farmers, irrigation managers, ag extension agents, water districts
Impact Evidence: Referenced in UC Cooperative Extension irrigation management guides for California
Notes: California-specific ET and climate monitoring; unique for CA agriculture; no direct substitute at this state-specific resolution

Function: Summaries and charts for SNOTEL snowpack data in partnership with NRCS.
User Segment: Water resource managers, hydrologists, municipalities, irrigation districts, NWS forecasters
Impact Evidence: Embedded in Western water supply forecast workflows; referenced in state water resource planning documents across CO, UT, WY, MT, ID, WA, OR
Notes: SNOTEL access interface in partnership with NRCS; NRCS hosts raw SNOTEL data nationally but WRCC provides Western synthesis and usability layer critical for water supply forecasting

Function: Hydrologic context and regional water resource summaries.
User Segment: Water resource managers, hydrologists, municipal water utilities, irrigation districts, planners
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Western water resource planning documents; used by Western states water agencies for drought context
Notes: Multi-source hydrologic context portal; unique Western water resource synthesis; no national equivalent integrating ACIS, USGS, and SNOTEL at this regional framing

Function: Daily and monthly snow analysis maps for the Western U.S.
User Segment: Water resource managers, NWS forecasters, hydrologists, emergency managers
Notes: Daily and monthly snow analysis maps; NRCS has national snow data but WRCC provides Western-specific map products and ACIS integration

Function: Query temperature, precipitation, and snowfall records for any station.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, state climatologists, educators, data analysts
Notes: Historical station data browser; ACIS-backed; standard data access tool for Western stations

Function: Visualizes long-term station trends and normals.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, policymakers, educators
Notes: Long-term trend visualization; NOAA Climate Explorer partial substitute; WRCC version Western-framed

Function: Displays average wind speed, direction, and gust climatologies.
User Segment: Wind energy developers, engineers, ranchers, fire managers, aviation
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Western wind energy site assessment workflows
Notes: Wind climatology viewer integrating ACIS and RAWS; no national equivalent at Western station resolution for wind direction and gust climatologies

Function: Lists station records and extremes by state and region.
User Segment: Researchers, NWS forecasters, media, emergency managers
Notes: Station records and extremes by state; NCEI has national data; WRCC interface provides Western-specific usability

Function: Real-time fire weather maps and RAWS integration for incident support.
User Segment: Fire managers, incident commanders, BLM/USFS staff, NWS fire weather forecasters, emergency managers
Impact Evidence: Embedded in NIFC fire weather operational workflows; referenced in BLM and USFS incident support protocols across Western states
Notes: Real-time fire weather portal with RAWS integration; RAWS-dependent not ACIS; unique Western fire weather infrastructure; loss would directly impact incident support operations

Function: Maps of forecasted fire danger indices for Western states.
User Segment: Fire managers, incident commanders, BLM/USFS staff, emergency managers, ranchers
Impact Evidence: Used by Western state fire agencies and NIFC for pre-positioning and resource allocation decisions
Notes: Fire danger forecast maps; NWS issues fire weather watches and warnings nationally but WRCC provides regional synthesis and mapped index products not replicated elsewhere

Function: Maps of heat index and risk categories for public health and emergency management.
User Segment: Public health agencies, emergency managers, outdoor workers, municipal planners
Impact Evidence: Referenced in Western state public health heat emergency guidance
Notes: Heat risk mapping tool; complements SERCC WBGT for Western region; NWS heat index exists nationally but WRCC provides interactive regional mapping layer

Function: Tracks moisture, temperature, and vegetative conditions across rangelands.
User Segment: Ranchers, BLM/USFS range managers, conservation districts, fire managers
Impact Evidence: Referenced in BLM rangeland condition monitoring protocols; embedded in Western range management workflows
Notes: Rangeland monitoring integrating RAWS and vegetation indices; unique Western infrastructure product; no national equivalent at this resolution and data integration

Function: California-specific station data, normals, and extremes.
User Segment: CA researchers, planners, water managers, ag extension agents, media
Impact Evidence: Referenced in CA state climate assessment documents; used by CA Department of Water Resources
Notes: California-specific climate portal; no substitute for CA-specific station normals and extremes at this resolution; highest-traffic specialty product in WRCC catalog

Function: Long-term climate normals displayed as maps by variable.
User Segment: Planners, researchers, educators, policymakers
Notes: Long-term climate normals as maps; PRISM and NCEI provide national normals; WRCC version Western-framed and user-facing

Function: Reports and data products for Pacific territories and island partners.
User Segment: Pacific territory planners, researchers, NWS Pacific forecasters, island resource managers
Impact Evidence: Only RCC product suite covering GU, AS, MP, and Pacific island territories; referenced in Pacific island climate resilience planning
Notes: Pacific Islands climate products are unique in the entire RCC network; no other center covers these territories; loss would eliminate the only regional climate service for Pacific island communities

Function: Monthly climate summaries for Western states.
User Segment: Planners, state agencies, researchers, media
Notes: Monthly Western climate narratives; staff-authored component limits automated restoration; maps are ACIS-recoverable

Function: Educational materials and outreach resources on climate and drought.
User Segment: K-12 educators, students, general public
Notes: Climate literacy materials; national substitutes exist; low unique restoration value

Function: Publications and technical reports on Western climate.
User Segment: Researchers, planners, policymakers
Impact Evidence: WRCC technical assessments cited in Western state climate adaptation plans
Notes: Technical reports and assessments; archivable as-is; high archival value for impact evidence base

Function: Seasonal climate outlooks and summaries of forecasted patterns.
User Segment: Farmers, water managers, planners, emergency managers, media
Notes: Seasonal climate outlooks for Western states; CPC provides official outlooks nationally; WRCC adds Western synthesis and regional framing

Function: Full archived interface with historical data pages and deprecated tools.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Pre-2022 portal archive; Wayback captures exist; mine for discontinued tool inventory

Function: Discontinued version of the RAWS data portal.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Discontinued RAWS interface; superseded by current RAWS portal; archival interest only

Function: Older static maps of precipitation and temperature anomalies.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Legacy static anomaly maps; superseded by current monitoring maps; archival interest only

Function: Early version of evapotranspiration and water balance visualization tool.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Early SCENIC ET portal; superseded by current SCENIC tool; useful for methodology lineage

Function: Discontinued fire weather mapping interfaces.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Discontinued fire weather pages; superseded by current Fire Weather Portal; archival interest only

Function: Archived SNOTEL summary interface prior to modernization.
User Segment: Researchers, historians
Notes: Archived SNOTEL interface; superseded by current SNOTEL access tool; archival interest only
